summary: This notebook demonstrates a two-stage retrieval-augmented generation pipeline for the EEDI competition, combining a quantized Qwen-2.5 32B model with a fine-tuned BGE sentence transformer. It first retrieves relevant mathematical misconceptions using metadata, feeds them to the LLM to generate a reasoned misconception, and then performs a second semantic search combining the LLM output with the original question to produce final predictions. The author shows that iterative context refinement and simple post-processing (handling serial number outputs) directly improved the leaderboard score from 0.362 to 0.368. Readers should understand how retrieval-augmented prompting can stabilize LLM reasoning on structured prediction tasks without fine-tuning, and how quantization plus vLLM enables efficient large-model inference on limited GPU resources.
competition_flow: Loads competition CSVs and a misconception mapping, performs two sequential semantic retrievals using a sentence transformer, generates LLM reasoning via vLLM, post-processes the output to map serial numbers back to IDs, and saves the final predictions as a CSV submission.
data_reading: pd.read_csv for train.csv, test.csv, and misconception_mapping.csv
pd.read_parquet for intermediate dataframes and labels
data_processing: Text normalization (lowercasing, URL removal, punctuation/whitespace standardization)
Regex-based extraction and cleaning of LLM responses
insights: Two-stage retrieval significantly improves LLM reasoning by first providing metadata-based context and then refining it with the model's own output.
Post-processing is critical when using LLMs for structured outputs, as they often default to referencing prompt indices instead of full text.
Quantized models combined with vLLM enable efficient large-model inference on limited GPU resources, though inference time remains substantial.
critical_findings: The LLM frequently outputs only the serial number of a retrieved misconception rather than the full text, requiring a fallback dictionary lookup during post-processing.
A simple parameter tweak (increasing max_tokens from 512 to 1024) directly improved the leaderboard score from 0.362 to 0.368.
